pi_items.source: Calculated using LC resonant frequency formula f = 1/(2π√(LC)) with L=10mH=0.01H and C=100nF=1e-7F, yielding f=5032.921210448703 Hz ≈ 5032.9 Hz

privileged_info_summary: This is a synthetic task where agents interact with an Android app to calculate the resonant frequency of an LC circuit. The critical calculation parameters are L=10mH and C=100nF. The expected resonant frequency is 5032.9 Hz, calculated using the formula f=1/(2π√(LC)). The task requires creating a result file at /sdcard/tasks/resonant_frequency_lc/result.txt with the calculated frequency value. Tolerance for success is ±5% (loose) or ±2% (tight) of the expected value. The underlying physics is correct: an LC circuit with these component values will have a resonant frequency of approximately 5033 Hz.
